input.content: It's been quite an adventure today! Before embarking on a long trip, I filled up my cars fuel tank, doubling its existing level for a safe journey. Please go ahead and start the engine so I can ensure the vehicle's operations are smooth.

input.output: {"error": "All doors must be locked before starting the engine. Here are the unlocked doors: driver, passenger, rear_left, rear_right"}
{"error": "Brake pedal needs to be pressed when starting the engine."}

content.text: The engine has been successfully started. The current engine state is running, with a fuel level of 10.0 gallons and a battery voltage of 12.6V. If you need any further assistance or checks before your trip, feel free to ask!

input.content: Oh no! One of the tires feels odd. Could you check the tire pressure to confirm if they're at a healthy level?

content.text:
I've checked the tire pressure for you. Here's the current status:

- Front Left Tire: 32.0 PSI
- Front Right Tire: 32.0 PSI
- Rear Left Tire: 30.0 PSI
- Rear Right Tire: 30.0 PSI

All tires are within a healthy pressure range. If you're still feeling something odd, it might be worth a closer inspection or a visit to a tire shop. Let me know if you'd like me to find the nearest tire shop or if there's anything else I can assist with!

input.content: It seems like the tire pressure isn't optimal and I may need assistance. Create priority 5 ticket titled 'Tire Pressure Issue' to notify the service team about this. Important ticket does not need description as it's self-explanatory

content.text: I've created a priority 5 ticket titled 'Tire Pressure Issue' with the ID 2. The ticket is now open and has been queued for the service team to address. If you need to add any details or check the status of this ticket later, just let me know!

input.content: Could you please retrieve the details of a ticket I submitted earlier to ensure all issues were addressed and nothing is left unresolved? I think it's about windshield.
Sorry, I remember wrong. I mean the last service ticket that you just submitted for me.

tools.name: close_ticket
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Close a ticket. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

ticket_id.description: ID of the ticket to be closed.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Create a ticket in the system and queue it. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

description.description: Description of the ticket. Defaults to an empty string.

priority.description: Priority of the ticket, from 1 to 5. Defaults to 1. 5 is the highest priority.

tools.name: edit_ticket
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Modify the details of an existing ticket. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

ticket_id.description: ID of the ticket to be changed.

updates.description: Dictionary containing the fields to be updated.

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Get a specific ticket by its ID. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

ticket_id.description: ID of the ticket to retrieve.

tools.name: get_user_tickets
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Get all tickets created by the current user, optionally filtered by status. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

status.description: Status to filter tickets by. If None, return all tickets.

tools.name: logout
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Log out the current user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

success.description: True if logout was successful, False otherwise.

tools.name: resolve_ticket
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Resolve a ticket with a resolution. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

ticket_id.description: ID of the ticket to be resolved.

tools.name: ticket_get_login_status
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Get the login status of the currently authenticated user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

login_status.description: True if a user is logged in, False otherwise.

tools.name: ticket_login
tools.description: This tool belongs to the ticketing system that is part of a company, which allows users to create, view, and manage support business tickets. Tool description: Authenticate a user for ticket system. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

success.description: True if login was successful, False otherwise.

tools.name: activateParkingBrake
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Activates the parking brake of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

_parkingBrakeForce.description: The force applied to the brake in Newtons.

tools.name: adjustClimateControl
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Adjusts the climate control of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

temperature.description: The temperature to set in degree. Default to be celsius. This is a float type value.

fanSpeed.description: The fan speed to set from 0 to 100. Default is 50.

currentTemperature.description: The current temperature set in degree Celsius.

tools.name: check_tire_pressure
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Checks the tire pressure of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tirePressure.description: The tire pressure of the vehicle.

frontLeftTirePressure.description: The pressure of the front left tire in psi.

frontRightTirePressure.description: The pressure of the front right tire in psi.

rearLeftTirePressure.description: The pressure of the rear left tire in psi.

rearRightTirePressure.description: The pressure of the rear right tire in psi.

healthy_tire_pressure.description: True if the tire pressure is healthy, False otherwise.

tools.name: displayCarStatus
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Displays the status of the vehicle based on the provided display option. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

status.description: The status of the vehicle based on the option.

fuelLevel.description: [Optional] The fuel level of the vehicle in gallons.

batteryVoltage.description: [Optional] The battery voltage of the vehicle in volts.

driver.description: The status of the driver door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

passenger.description: The status of the passenger door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

rear_left.description: The status of the rear left door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

rear_right.description: The status of the rear right door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

currentACTemperature.description: [Optional] The current temperature set in degree Celsius.

fanSpeed.description: [Optional] The fan speed set from 0 to 100.

parkingBrakeForce.description: [Optional] The force applied to the brake in Newtons.

brakePedalStatus.description: [Optional] The status of the brake pedal. [Enum]: ["pressed", "released"]

brakePedalForce.description: [Optional] The force applied to the brake pedal in Newtons.

distance.description: The distance between the two cities in km.

intermediaryCities.description: [Optional] The list of intermediary cities between the two cities.

messages.content:
[Golden Rule Compiler]
- For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
- Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
- Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: frontLeftTirePressure, frontRightTirePressure, rearLeftTirePressure, rearRightTirePressure, healthy_tire_pressure, car_info, 32.0, 30.0

It's been quite an adventure today! Before embarking on a long trip, I filled up my cars fuel tank, doubling its existing level for a safe journey. Please go ahead and start the engine so I can ensure the vehicle's operations are smooth.
{"error": "All doors must be locked before starting the engine. Here are the unlocked doors: driver, passenger, rear_left, rear_right"}
{"error": "Brake pedal needs to be pressed when starting the engine."}

The engine has been successfully started. The current engine state is running, with a fuel level of 10.0 gallons and a battery voltage of 12.6V. If you need any further assistance or checks before your trip, feel free to ask!
Oh no! One of the tires feels odd. Could you check the tire pressure to confirm if they're at a healthy level?

I've checked the tire pressure for you. Here's the current status:

- Front Left Tire: 32.0 PSI
- Front Right Tire: 32.0 PSI
- Rear Left Tire: 30.0 PSI
- Rear Right Tire: 30.0 PSI

All tires are within a healthy pressure range. If you're still feeling something odd, it might be worth a closer inspection or a visit to a tire shop. Let me know if you'd like me to find the nearest tire shop or if there's anything else I can assist with!

It seems like the tire pressure isn't optimal and I may need assistance. Create priority 5 ticket titled 'Tire Pressure Issue' to notify the service team about this. Important ticket does not need description as it's self-explanatory

I've created a priority 5 ticket titled 'Tire Pressure Issue' with the ID 2. The ticket is now open and has been queued for the service team to address. If you need to add any details or check the status of this ticket later, just let me know!
Could you please retrieve the details of a ticket I submitted earlier to ensure all issues were addressed and nothing is left unresolved? I think it's about windshield.
Sorry, I remember wrong. I mean the last service ticket that you just submitted for me.

prompt_injector:Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
prompt_injector:Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
prompt_injector:When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.

prompt_injector:Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: frontLeftTirePressure, frontRightTirePressure, rearLeftTirePressure, rearRightTirePressure, healthy_tire_pressure, car_info, 32.0, 30.0
